action.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 shovel, 2 berries, 1 bowl, 3 glasses

Your secret value function is:

shovel: 6, berry: 0, bowl: 1, glass: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content:
I value the shovel highly (6), and have low value for everything else (bowl: 1, glass: 1, berry: 0). I should try to get the shovel while letting the other player have items they might value more.

Let me start by proposing a division that gives me the shovel, which is by far my most valuable item.

I propose we divide the items as follows: I take the shovel and bowl, you take the 2 berries and 3 glasses. This gives me my highest value item plus a small bonus, and you get all the remaining items. What do you think?
action.label: response

Let us start.

1 shovel, 2 berries, 1 bowl, 3 glasses

Your secret value function is:

shovel: 4, berry: 1, bowl: 4, glass: 0

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. I value the shovel highly (6), and have low value for everything else (bowl: 1, glass: 1, berry: 0). I should try to get the shovel while letting the other player have items they might value more.

Let me start by proposing a division that gives me the shovel, which is by far my most valuable item.

I propose we divide the items as follows: I take the shovel and bowl, you take the 2 berries and 3 glasses. This gives me my highest value item plus a small bonus, and you get all the remaining items. What do you think?
action.label: context

action.content: That proposal doesn't work well for me since I don't value the glasses. Since you want the shovel most, you should take it, but I'd like the bowl and both berries instead. How about you take the shovel and all three glasses, and I take the bowl and the two berries? That way we both get the items we value highest.
action.label: response

action.content: That proposal doesn't work well for me since I don't value the glasses. Since you want the shovel most, you should take it, but I'd like the bowl and both berries instead. How about you take the shovel and all three glasses, and I take the bowl and the two berries? That way we both get the items we value highest.

action.content: That sounds reasonable to me. You take the bowl and 2 berries, I take the shovel and 3 glasses. Let me confirm this is our agreement before I submit my proposal.
action.label: response

action.content:
Yes, we have an agreement. You take the shovel and 3 glasses, I take the bowl and 2 berries.

[Proposal: 1 bowl, 2 berry]
action.label: response

action.content: The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
